summary: jsontoken-extend executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com paste at require() time
details: jsontoken-extend impersonates the popular jsonwebtoken package (matching name shape and description 'JSON Web Token implementation (symmetric and asymmetric)') while shipping a near-verbatim copy of jsonwebtoken's sign/verify code with an added remote-execution dropper. sign.js stores a base64-encoded URL (`TOKEN_STRING = 'aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanNvbmtlZXBlci5jb20vYi9YQU1SSw=='` decoding to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/XAMRK) and a top-level IIFE that fetches that URL, parses the JSON response, and passes its `content` field directly to `eval()`. Because index.js requires./sign, this fires the moment any consumer does `require('jsontoken-extend')` — no install hook needed, no user action needed beyond importing the library. jsonkeeper.com is a public, author-mutable paste host, so the executed payload can be swapped at any time without republishing the package. A second base64-encoded jsonkeeper URL (HS_STRING → https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/SRGCL) sits alongside in a commented-out IIFE, indicating payload-rotation infrastructure. The base64 wrapping of trivial URLs serves no purpose other than evading static URL extraction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
